rulings.Case Title: RICKY SU VS. SUSAN C. NGOON ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel The Depositions Of Defendants Susan C. Ngoon, Trustee Of The Ngoon Family Trust And Kyin K. Ngoon With Production Of Documents And Sanctions Against Defendants And Their Attorney On Shortened Time
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for March 2, 2021 line 2. PLAINTIFF RICKY SU's Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel The Depositions Of Defendants Susan C. Ngoon, Trustee Of The Ngoon Family Trust And Kyin K. Ngoon With Production Of Documents And Sanctions Against Defendants And Their Attorney On Shortened Time is GRANTED. Depositions of defendant Susan C. Ngoon and Kyin K. Ngoon, shall be set on mutually agreeable date prior to the March 8, 2021. If parties cannot agree on a date, the depositions shall proceed on March 3 and 4, 2021. If defendant Kyin K. Ngoon is unable to attend his noticed deposition due to a mental health diagnosis that prevents his giving sworn testimony, than Kyin K. Ngoon's appointed guardian ad litem so state this fact on the record at the time and place of deposition. Documents must be produced at the deposition consistent with November 24, 2020 Order. Hearing Required to address sanctions. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303). Zoom appearance is preferred. Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: 2400 PACIFIC AVENUE, LLC VS. MATTHEW A. BENNETT ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Reconsideration Of Demurrer To Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for March 2, 2021 line 5. PLAINTIFF 2400 PACIFIC AVENUE, LLC'S Notice Of Motion And Motion For Reconsideration Of Demurrer To Complaint is DENIED. Actions for collection of COVID19 rental debt shall not be commenced before August 1, 2021. At that time the landlord will have a choice to file it as Small Claims or as general civil action. Both types of actions will be handled by the Small Claims department (for each type of case to be governed by appropriate procedural rules). Therefore, the demurrer to the complaint filed on September 15, 2020 for recovery of April 2020- August 2020 rent is sustained at this time. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303). Zoom appearance is preferred. Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: VIRGINIA MERAZA-SANTOS VS. SARA MAE SWEENEY ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for March 2, 2021 line 7. Plaintiff Virgina Meraza-Santos' Motion For Summary Judgment Or, In The Alternative, For Summary Adjudication In Favor Of Plaintiff is GRANTED. Moving party shifted its burden. Defendant failed to create a triable issue of material fact with competent admissible evidence. The Court notes that Defendant failed to file a motion to vacate discovery order deeming admissions admitted. If the Court were to consider Defendant's February 25, 2021 Declaration as an attempt of such motion, there is insufficient evidence to grant a motion to vacate. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303). Zoom appearance is preferred. Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.
